title: Scripture
description: Fixes inconsistencies with Minecraft's font while upscaling it to 32x.
body:
![Scripture Title](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/fb095740fd520c2b8694234adeaca1ba5c8097f8.png)

Scripture is an enhanced font pack with five standards:
### Modernization
One of Scripture’s goals is to modernize Minecraft’s font. Its style has remained mostly unchanged since release. This pack updates it with a simpler, more consistent design that improves readability and simplicity.
### Legibility
Even when upscaled in Faithful, Minecraft’s font can still lack legibility. Scripture redesigns several characters to make their features clearer and easier to distinguish.
### Refinement
Mojang’s font suffers from alignment, spacing, and smoothing issues. Some characters are inconsistently sized, overly pixelated, or too similar. Scripture fixes this by hand-editing each font texture to refine spacing, alignment, and smoothing.
### Coverage
Unlike many font packs, Scripture aims for full coverage. It redesigns not only ASCII but also accented characters, non-Latin European glyphs, and illager and enchanting table ASCII, covering characterss others often ignore.
### Faithfulness
Scripture’s final standard is faithfulness. While modernizing the font, it remains true to the original style. The goal is improving readability and consistency without losing the familiar look of Vanilla.
### Creation
Your first thought seeing the faithfulness standard was most likely the Faithful resource pack. You'd be right! Scripture was built off of the font set from Faithful 32x and then modified to meet standards.
### Credits
I must give them where they're due.

Faithful 32x (base):

[![Faithful 32x Redirect](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/faithful-Resource-Pack/Branding/main/wordmarks/outlined/cracked/f32_wordmark.png)](https://modrinth.com/resourcepack/faithful-32x)
